summary: Completed comprehensive testing of observability layer for Next.js Cloudflare Pages project. All structured logging, cost tracking, environment handling, health checks, and API integrations are working correctly. TypeScript compilation passes with only the pre-existing waitlist error.
passed_tests: TypeScript Compilation: Only pre-existing waitlist/page.tsx error present
lib/logger.ts: LogEvent interface exports correctly with required fields (level, event, timestamp)
lib/logger.ts: log() function produces JSON output with proper structure
lib/logger.ts: logRequest() calculates durationMs correctly using Date.now() - startTime
lib/logger.ts: logError() handles Error objects, strings, and unknown objects properly
lib/logger.ts: createTimer() returns function that calculates ms elapsed since creation
lib/cost-tracker.ts: RequestCost interface has all required fields (userId, model, tokens, costs, timestamp)
lib/cost-tracker.ts: COST_CONSTANTS.TTS_COST_PER_CHAR = 0.0000003 as specified
lib/cost-tracker.ts: calculateTotalCost() returns correct fields with Gemini model costs integration
lib/cost-tracker.ts: DAILY_BUDGET_USD defaults to 5.0 with environment override support
lib/cost-tracker.ts: checkBudgetAlert() returns true when over budget and logs warn event
lib/env.ts: getEnv() throws clear error with key name when env var missing
lib/env.ts: envExists() returns boolean correctly for existing/missing/empty vars
app/api/health/route.ts: HealthResponse interface with correct shape (status/version/checks/timestamp)
app/api/health/route.ts: Returns 200 for ok, 207 for degraded, 503 for down status codes
app/api/health/route.ts: Never exposes actual env values (only npm_package_version)
middleware.ts: Rate limiting still works with IP-based burst protection
middleware.ts: Logging events api.request/api.rate_limited/api.unauthorized are logged
middleware.ts: Health route excluded from rate limiting and auth
app/api/chat/route.ts: createTimer() used, calculateTotalCost() called after stream, checkBudgetAlert() called
app/api/tts/route.ts: Logging of tts.request with charCount and durationMs
app/api/memory/route.ts: Logging of memory.read/memory.write with factCount
All API routes: logError called on errors with proper error handling
